channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Ceasefire disputes between Israel and Hamas, Hezbollah throw region into turmoil
item.description: The ceasefire agreement between Israel, Hamas and Hezbollah is in new jeopardy Sunday as various groups slow walk responsibilities under the deal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats are splintered on immigration and how to respond to Trump
item.description: The Democratic Party has so far been splintered in responding to President Donald Trump’s push against illegal immigration.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Groves, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump's border emergency declaration comes amid relative calm after years of major turmoil
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Long stretches of silence on a Border Patrol scanner are punctuated with updates on tracking a single migrant for hours. The radio traffic sounds like a throwback to earlier times, before the United States became the largest destination for asylum-seekers in 2017.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, GREGORY BULL and EUGENE GARCIA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli Columbia professor wants Trump to block certain institutions from receiving federal funding
item.description: Columbia associate professor activist Shai Davidai is calling on Trump to stop private institutions from receiving federal funds if they hire professors who support terror groups.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Just days into the new Trump administration, worries spike amid a show of force on immigration
item.description: NEWARK, N.J. (AP) — Less than a week into his presidency, the Trump administration touted deportation efforts and published new rules Friday making it easier to remove people — part of a flurry of actions to make good on campaign promises to crack down on illegal immigration.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI and REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hegseth says Pentagon will 'put America first' as sec. of defense
item.description: Vice President JD Vance's tiebreaking vote confirmed Pete Hegseth as secretary of defense under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Senate forces rare weekend vote to push through Kristi Noem as Homeland Security chief
item.description: The Senate will meet for a rare session on Saturday to vote on whether to confirm Gov. Kristi Noem as the director of Homeland Security.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump proposes 'getting rid of FEMA' while touring disaster areas
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — President Donald Trump surveyed disaster zones in California and North Carolina on Friday and said he was considering “getting rid of” the Federal Emergency Management Agency, offering the latest sign of how he is weighing sweeping changes to the nation's central organization for
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, CHRIS MEGERIAN and MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pete Hegseth confirmed to lead Pentagon after VP Vance casts tie-breaking vote
item.description: The Senate voted Friday night on the confirmation of Trump's pick to lead the Department of Defense, Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: State Department freezes new funding for nearly all US aid programs worldwide
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The State Department ordered a sweeping freeze Friday on new funding for almost all U.S. foreign assistance, making exceptions for emergency food programs and military aid to Israel and Egypt. The order threatened a quick halt to many of the billions of dollars in U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: St. Paul City Council President Mitra Jalali announces resignation, citing health concerns
item.description: After nearly seven years with the city, St. Paul council member and president Mitra Jalali has decided to step down. She says she is focusing on prioritizing her mental and physical health.
item.dc:creator: Sam Stroozas, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court will weigh approval for US’ 1st publicly funded religious charter school, in Oklahoma
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court agreed Friday to take on a new culture war dispute: whether the nation’s first publicly funded religious charter school should be allowed to open in Oklahoma.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What JFK assassination files are still classified? Trump’s order could bring them to light
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Millions of documents related to the 1963 assassination of President John F. Kennedy in Dallas have already been made public, but President Donald Trump has ordered the release of thousands of still-classified files.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance's tiebreaker vote confirms Pete Hegseth as Defense secretary
item.description: Vance cast the tiebreaker vote after Sen. Mitch McConnell, R-Ky., voted no for Hegseth, leaving the chamber at a 50-50 tie.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Republican proposes amendment to allow Trump to serve third term
item.description: Like other U.S. presidents, Trump is barred from running for a third White House term by the 22nd Amendment to the Constitution.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump signs order directing release of JFK assassination files
item.description: The order directs the director of national intelligence and attorney general to present a plan within 15 days for the “full and complete release of records relating to the assassination of President John F. Kennedy.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada state senator takes on gender, sports issue at UNLV, UNR, community colleges
item.description: Republican state Senator Carrie Buck is sponsoring legislation that would require Nevada colleges and universities to restrict participation in athletics based on the athlete's gender at birth.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump calls DEI programs ‘illegal’ and ‘immoral.’ Here’s how he’s ending them
item.description: President Donald Trump has issued sweeping executive actions swiftly ending diversity, equity, inclusion and accessibility programs within the federal government. Already, the work is underway.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: Stick with us: Why the internet is obsessed with one of the simplest tools known to man
item.description: "Stick Nation" is a worldwide movement that thrives on irreverent, fantastical and downright wholesome descriptions of sticks.
item.dc:creator: Alina Hartounian, MPR News

item.title: Everything Trump did in the first executive orders and actions of his presidency
item.description: President Donald Trump began his second administration with a blitz of policy actions to reorient U.S. government priorities. Some have an immediate policy impact. Others are more symbolic.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: How Trump's gender order impacts Californians' passports, driver's licenses
item.description: President Donald Trump's executive order declaring the U.S. only recognizes two genders, male and female, has raised questions about what this means for the validity of documents distributed to transgender Californians with their preferred gender marker.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Congress clears GOP-led immigration enforcement bill, with Democrats on board
item.description: The Laken Riley Act would make it easier for federal immigration officials to detain and deport those without legal status who are charged with specific crimes.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo and Deirdre Walsh, MPR News

item.title: Trump pumps brakes on Day 1 tariffs
item.description: President Donald Trump pumped the brakes on his plan to deliver wide-ranging tariffs as soon as he took office, slow-tracking and toning down changes to the U.S. trade system that figured as a centerpiece of his campaign.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Inauguration prayer service's pastor asks Trump to 'have mercy' on transgender kids, immigrants
item.description: “I ask you to have mercy upon the people in our country who are scared. There are gay, lesbian, transgender children, Democratic, Republican, independent families — some who fear for their lives,” she said.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Trump rolls back Biden directive to study methods of lowering prescription costs
item.description: Among the dozens of Biden-era executive orders he rescinded on day one, President Trump nixed one that aimed to explore new health care payment models that would lower prescription costs for Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: New York plan would give money to new moms: Who qualifies
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York would give hundreds of dollars in financial support to low-income parents under a new proposal. Last week, Gov. Kathy Hochul announced a proposal to give low-income parents a “birth allowance” during pregnancy and at the birth of their child.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Trump wants to pull US out of World Health Organization again; here's what may happen next
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S. President Donald Trump used one of the flurry of executive actions that he issued on his first day back in the White House to begin the process of withdrawing the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Maria Cheng, WFLA, NBC
